page: Amazon Web Services m8gn.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and custom AWS Graviton processors. Built on the 64-bit ARMv9 architecture, the instance features 4 dedicated physical cores utilizing the Neoverse-V2 design, operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or discrete GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and EBS optimization, with a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average performance across standard compute and memory tasks, with notable strengths in single-threaded compression. The instance achieves a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 30.3 GB/sec and a single-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 14.41 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% of tested hardware. Standard multi-core operations, such as stress-ng multi-core CPU performance (6817.06) and Geekbench multi-core (5917.0), fall within the middle 50% tier. However, the hardware exhibits limitations in cryptographic and extended instruction workloads, with the Passmark CPU encryption test scoring in the bottom 10% (753.68) and the Passmark CPU score placing in the bottom 25%. LLM inference capabilities are also limited, with text generation on a medium 7B model registering a weak 6.53 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8gn.xlarge offers a balanced resource profile for general-purpose workloads that do not require local storage or specialized GPU acceleration. The dedicated ARM64 core allocation provides predictable execution times for web serving, database operations, and software compilation. It is suited for static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 9.78 GB/sec, and Redis key-value stores, demonstrating 9.54 million SET operations per second. The instance is less suitable for heavy cryptographic processing, complex vector calculations, or large-scale machine learning inference.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8gn.xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by a 2.8 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor built on the ARMv9 architecture. It features 4 dedicated physical cores, 16.0 GB of system memory, and a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong single-threaded compression capabilities, with gzip compression reaching 30.3 GB/sec. However, the instance exhibits weak performance in cryptographic tasks, scoring in the bottom 10% on Passmark encryption tests, and limited LLM inference speeds. It is optimized for balanced workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, and Redis database operations, where it delivers average, predictable throughput without the overhead of x86 licensing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 4 dedicated Graviton cores, 16 GB RAM, and 12 Gbps network speed, showing strong compression but weak cryptographic performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated Graviton cores, 16 GB RAM, and 12 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated physical cores, balanced memory allocation, and strong single-threaded compression performance for network-optimized workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated ARM64 Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores at 2.8 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor optimization
Strong single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression performance
Weak performance in cryptographic operations and extended instruction sets
Suited for static web serving, software compilation, and Redis databases
